philippines.title: Eastern Samar town bans butchering carabaos as farm fuel costs rise
philippines.description:
TACLOBAN CITY — The municipal government of Sulat, Eastern Samar has prohibited the slaughter of carabaos for fear of losing the traditional farm machinery alternative amid rising fuel costs. In an interview on Tuesday, Sulat Mayor Javier Zacate said he issued an executive order (EO) prohibiting the butchering of carabaos. The order covers instances during fiestas and other celebrations to ensure agricultural sustainability. Zacate noted that the policy was shaped through extensive consultations, including small-group discussions and a Municipal Development Council (MDC) meeting. The meeting was aimed at addressing concerns over rising diesel prices impacting local farmers. “There are no clear […]...
Keep on reading: Eastern Samar town bans butchering carabaos as farm fuel costs rise

philippines.title: Cebu International School hosts free public forum on Artificial Intelligence
philippines.description:
On March 26, 2026, Cebu International School (CIS) opened its doors to the public for a timely forum on Artificial Intelligence (AI) at Oakridge Horizons, Oakridge Business Park, Cebu City.  Led by CIS Superintendent Peter Welch, the free event gathered members of the community from parents and educators to business leaders and students for a meaningful discussion on transformative innovation and its effects on the general public. Welch’s talk started by providing a background on where AI is at the moment, its characteristics, and what it is becoming. He delivered a balanced discussion on the emerging opportunities and possible challenges […]...
Keep on reading: Cebu International School hosts free public forum on Artificial Intelligence

philippines.title: Grammy-winning singer-songwriter Ray Stevens recovering after breaking neck
philippines.description:
NASHVILLE, Tennessee — Grammy-winning singer-songwriter Ray Stevens broke his neck and is recovering at home after being briefly hospitalized, according to a statement published Tuesday, April 7. The 87-year-old country star, who is known for his topical satire, fell late last month, according to the statement released on his X account. He will need to wear a neck brace for about a month. “He remains fully mobile & in good spirits,” the statement said. Stevens’ decades-long recording career has included such hits as the Grammy-winning “Everything Is Beautiful” as well as the zany “The Streak,” which captured the mid-1970s craze […]...
Keep on reading: Grammy-winning singer-songwriter Ray Stevens recovering after breaking neck

energy.title: Ceasefire Sparks Tentative Revival of Work on Qatar LNG Project
energy.description: Japan’s engineering giant Chiyoda Corporation is weighing resuming work on a massive expansion at Qatar’s LNG export site at Ras Laffan after the U.S. and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire. Chiyoda is one of the contractors working on the North Field East (NFE) LNG project expansion, one of Qatar’s huge projects to boost its export capacity over the coming years. However, the war in the Middle East and Iranian missile strikes at Ras Laffan last month halted production and exports, as well as expansion works. The ceasefire has…

energy.title: China’s Teapot Refiners Rush to Secure Iranian Crude as Oil Prices Slide
energy.description: China’s independent refiners are looking for prompt Iranian crude cargos after Beijing issued the latest batch of quotas for oil imports and after international prices took a plunge following the latest news about the war in the Middle East. Brent crude and WTI are both down, below $100 per barrel, after President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire, with Iran saying it would ensure safe passage for tankers via the Strait of Hormuz. Amid these developments, the so-called teapot refiners are eager to stock up on Iranian crude, unnamed traders…
